title: Last Chad Standing: Inside the World-First MMA Play-to-Earn Game
html:
Last Chad Standing is an MMA play to earn game built by DeHub: a fighter battle royale featuring licensed mixed martial artists, where surviving the cage earns you on-chain rewards in the ecosystem token, $DHB. It sits at the centre of the DeHub arcade as the flagship title, and it occupies a niche almost nobody else has claimed properly, combat sports wired directly into a Web3 economy. If you have been looking for a genuine crypto MMA game rather than another reskinned card battler, this is the one to understand.

Below we break down what the game actually is, how the play-to-earn model works, why the people behind it have the credibility to build it, and how you get involved through the DeHub app.

What is Last Chad Standing?

Last Chad Standing is a battle-royale MMA game. Rather than a one-on-one bout, a roster of fighters is dropped into a shrinking field and the last competitor still standing takes the win, hence the name. The format borrows the tension of the battle-royale genre, the elimination-by-elimination squeeze, and marries it to the striking, grappling and stamina management of mixed martial arts.

The headline detail is that the fighters are licensed. This is not a generic set of interchangeable avatars; it is built around real mixed martial artists with the rights secured, which is what separates a serious blockchain fighting game from the flood of anonymous asset flips. We are not going to name specific fighters or promise a release date here, because those announcements belong to the official channels, but the licensing model is the point: recognisable combatants, a real sport, real stakes.

As the DeHub flagship title, it is not a standalone app bolted on the side. It is woven into the wider platform, the same account, the same wallet, the same $DHB token that powers uploads, subscriptions and tips across DeHub, the decentralised social network. Winning in the cage feeds the same on-chain balance you use everywhere else in the ecosystem.

How the MMA battle royale play-to-earn model works

The core loop of any mma battle royale play to earn title is simple to state: you play, you perform, you earn. What matters is how honestly that loop is built, and DeHub's design leans on the same principles that run the rest of the platform, transparency and on-chain settlement.

Here is the shape of it:

  • You compete. You take a fighter into the battle royale and try to outlast the field. Outcomes turn on skill, timing and decision-making rather than on who spent the most before the match started.
  • You earn $DHB. Performance converts into rewards denominated in the ecosystem token. Because $DHB lives primarily on Base, an Ethereum Layer 2, the rewards settle on-chain rather than sitting as points in a private ledger the operator can quietly rewrite.
  • You keep what you earn. The token is a real asset. It is tradable on decentralised exchanges and on Coinbase, and it is listed on CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap, so what you take out of the arcade is not locked inside a walled garden.

We are deliberately not quoting prize pools, reward rates or tokenomics figures, because those move and because inventing them would do you a disservice. The concept is what counts: a crypto MMA game where the reward layer is a public, tradable token, not an internal scoreboard.

If you want the broader theory of how earning-while-playing models are meant to work, and where they go wrong, our explainer on watch-to-earn versus play-to-earn versus move-to-earn sets Last Chad Standing in context alongside the rest of the X-to-earn landscape.

Why the founders can actually build this

Plenty of projects slap "play-to-earn" on a combat game. Very few have founders with real footing in both fighting culture and mainstream entertainment. DeHub was co-founded by Mike Hales and Indi Jay Cammish, who also built First Class Agency, the UK's number-one official TikTok LIVE partner agency, representing more than 1,200 live streamers.

That matters for two reasons. First, they understand combat sports and live entertainment as a business, not as a marketing theme, which is exactly the muscle you need to secure fighter licensing and build something fans recognise. Second, they understand audiences at scale, how to attract creators, keep them and reward attention, which is the connective tissue between a game and a social platform. DeHub also sponsors the North Wales Crusaders rugby league club, so the sporting connection runs through the whole operation rather than being confined to one title.

The company itself is DeLabs Ltd, a United Kingdom company; the project was founded in 2021 with the UK entity incorporated in 2022. This is not an anonymous team behind a pseudonymous contract, and for anyone weighing up whether a crypto game is worth their time, a named UK company with a real track record is a meaningful signal.

Where Last Chad Standing fits in the DeHub ecosystem

The game is one pillar of a much larger open-source, user-owned platform. Alongside the play-to-earn arcade, DeHub runs a chronological, transparent social feed with no opaque algorithm, tokenised uploads, on-chain tradable subscriptions, watch-to-earn bounties, live streaming on Livepeer that has scaled beyond 50,000 concurrent viewers, and open-source end-to-end encrypted messaging.

The advantage of that integration is that Last Chad Standing does not have to bootstrap its own economy from nothing. It plugs into an existing token, an existing audience of creators and viewers, and an existing on-chain infrastructure, including a custom CDN with sub-200ms global latency, which is exactly the kind of backbone a real-time fighting game needs. Your $DHB earned in the cage can be spent supporting creators, unlocking token-gated content or tipping a live stream, all inside the same account.

It also means the game is not the only way to earn on the platform. If Last Chad Standing is your entry point, you will find the wider ecosystem rewards attention and participation across the board, which is worth understanding before you decide how much time to put in.

How to get involved

Getting started is straightforward:

  1. Get the DeHub app. Head to the DeHub app on the web, or download it from Google Play. One account gives you the feed, the arcade and your wallet.
  2. Set up your wallet and $DHB. The arcade runs on the ecosystem token. If you are in Britain and new to buying it, our guide to acquiring the token walks through the process end to end.
  3. Follow the official channels for the game. Roster reveals, launch timing and event details are announced on DeHub's X account and Telegram. We are not going to pre-empt those dates here, so the official feeds are where to watch.

If you want to line up other titles to play alongside it, our roundup of free play-to-earn games with no investment in 2026 is a sensible next read, and if you are earning in the UK, do read our breakdown of play-to-earn earnings and HMRC tax before you cash anything out, because crypto rewards can carry tax consequences and the sensible move is to keep records from day one.

The best crypto battle royale games have a moat, and this one is combat sports

When people search for the best crypto battle royale games, most of what turns up is generic fantasy or sci-fi shooters with a token bolted on. The MMA and fighting-sports corner of play-to-earn is, frankly, wide open. Last Chad Standing is aiming squarely at that gap, a licensed combat-sports battle royale with founders who have genuine entertainment and fighting-industry credibility behind them, all inside a live, user-owned social platform rather than a single-purpose game with an uncertain future.

That combination, real sport, real licensing, a real tradable token and a real company, is what gives this mma play to earn game a moat that a hastily assembled clone cannot copy. The niche is there for the taking, and DeHub is the one taking it.

Ready to step into the cage? Start with the DeHub app and follow the ecosystem token across the whole platform.

Frequently asked questions

What is Last Chad Standing?

Last Chad Standing is DeHub's flagship MMA play to earn game: a battle royale featuring licensed mixed martial artists, where the last fighter standing wins and performance converts into on-chain rewards in the ecosystem token, $DHB. It is part of the wider DeHub play-to-earn arcade rather than a standalone release.

Is Last Chad Standing free to play?

DeHub runs a play-to-earn arcade with free-to-play titles, and the platform itself is free to join, with uploads that can be free, pay-per-view or token-gated. Specific entry mechanics for individual matches or tournaments are announced through DeHub's official channels, so check the DeHub app, X and Telegram for the current details rather than relying on figures quoted elsewhere.

What blockchain does the game run on?

The DeHub ecosystem token, $DHB, runs primarily on Base, an Ethereum Layer 2, with contracts also deployed on BNB Chain and Polygon. Rewards earned in the game settle on-chain in $DHB, which is tradable on decentralised exchanges and on Coinbase, and listed on CoinGecko and CoinMarketCap.

How do you earn from an MMA play to earn game?

You earn by competing and performing well, outlasting the field in the battle royale converts into $DHB rewards that settle to your wallet on-chain. Because the token is a real, tradable asset rather than internal points, what you earn can be traded or spent across the DeHub platform. If you are earning in the UK, read our play-to-earn and HMRC tax guide first.
